description: Unified browser automation tool for web navigation and interaction

goto.when_to_use: When you need to visit a specific web page

go_back.when_to_use: When you need to return to the previous page

click.description: Click an element on the page

parameters.fallback_instruction: string (optional, natural language fallback if selector fails)

click.when_to_use: When you need to interact with a button, link, or clickable element

type_text.description: Type text into an input field
type_text.when_to_use: When you need to fill in a form field or search box

secure_fill.description: Securely fill a credential field without exposing value to LLM context

parameters.field_description: string (description of the field, e.g. 'password input')

secure_fill.when_to_use: When entering passwords, tokens, or sensitive credentials

screenshot.description: Capture a screenshot of the current page

parameters.full_page: boolean (default: false, set true for full page capture)

screenshot.when_to_use: When you need to visually inspect the page or save evidence

observe.description: Get DOM tree and page state as structured data including URL, title, headings, links, buttons, inputs, and interactive elements
observe.when_to_use: When you need to understand what is on the page before acting. Always observe before clicking or typing.

get_page_source.description: Get the raw HTML source of the current page
get_page_source.when_to_use: When you need the full HTML for detailed parsing or analysis

extract_links.description: Get all hyperlinks on the current page with their href, text, and title
extract_links.when_to_use: When you need a raw list of all links available on the page

explore_astar.description: Rank page hyperlinks using A* heuristic to find the best path to a target. Scores links based on text relevance, keyword matches, and URL depth.

parameters.target: string (required, what you are looking for)
parameters.keyword_weights: object (optional, keyword to weight map, e.g. {"security": 10, "admin": 8})
parameters.top_k: integer (default: 5, number of top-ranked links to return)

explore_astar.when_to_use: When you need to discover which link on the page is most likely to lead to your goal. Use before navigating to pick the best link.

find_path.description: Depth-1 lookahead scout. Opens each top-ranked link in a new tab, scores the content of each destination page, and returns the best URL with reasoning.

parameters.target: string (required, what you are looking for)

find_path.when_to_use: When explore_astar is not confident enough and you need to actually visit candidate pages to score them before committing to a path

wait_for_selector.description: Wait for an element to appear on the page

parameters.timeout_ms: integer (default: 5000, max wait time in milliseconds)

wait_for_selector.when_to_use: When a page is loading and you need to wait for specific content to appear before proceeding

tabs_list.description: List all open browser tabs with their IDs, URLs, and active status
tabs_list.when_to_use: When you need to know what tabs are open

tab_create.description: Open a new browser tab, optionally navigating to a URL

parameters.url: string (optional, URL to open in the new tab)

tab_create.when_to_use: When you need to open a page without leaving the current one

tab_switch.description: Switch to a specific browser tab by its ID

parameters.tab_id: string (required, the tab ID from tabs_list)

tab_switch.when_to_use: When you need to go back to a previously opened tab

tab_close.description: Close a browser tab by its ID

parameters.tab_id: string (required, the tab ID to close)

tab_close.when_to_use: When a tab is no longer needed and should be cleaned up

analyze_vision.description: Use vision AI to analyze what is visually displayed on the page, including layout, images, and elements that may not be in the DOM

parameters.prompt: string (optional, specific aspect to focus the analysis on)

analyze_vision.when_to_use: When DOM observation is not enough and you need visual understanding of the page

extract_settings.description: Extract structured settings and configuration data from the current page using DOM-first extraction with vision AI fallback

parameters.saas_name: string (required, name of the SaaS application)
parameters.schema: object (optional, expected settings schema with labels and keys)

extract_settings.when_to_use: When you need to extract security settings or configuration data from a SaaS admin page
